interactions.body:
(CB CANCEL - Specific email subject lines) [RE:Your Transform CreditBuilder application] Hi Scottie,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $10000 loan on 07/15/2024. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as payment for a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, your CreditBuilder account is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, TransformCredit can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Transform CreditBuilder here - transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ . However, I can confirm that I have canceled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. 

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 750+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. Follow this link to pick up where you left off: https://tcrdt.com/s/SBDT5W5U

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
Jade @ Transform CreditBuilder

interactions.body: (Requesting lower loan without a GT SMS) Hi Ariela, Together Loans requires a cosigner, regardless of the requested loan amount. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you and that we should too, regardless of your credit score. A suitable cosigner is someone who has a very good credit history with no trouble paying bills in the past, and who can afford to make the monthly payments if you don't. Although they don't have to be a homeowner, it may increase the possibility of us accepting them as your cosigner if they do own their own home. If you need to update your loan amount or term, you can do so here: https://tcrdt.com/s/SORGPGY9 Thanks, Together Loans

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Courtney, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/YNDFE9IW - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Transform Credit

Hi TILA, Great news - we're able to lend you $3000! You just need someone to cosign for you. A cosigner is someone you know who: - Is over 23 - Has a credit score of more than 750.- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time. - Trusts you and will pay if you do not - Will give correct contact details and sign the agreementMost people ask a family member or a friend as they know you best and trust you the most. interactions.body:
(Borrower GT Default Email) [RE:Affordability Confirmation] Hi Monica, 

Here at Transform Credit, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 750
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://transformcredit.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/NNJ5Z1TF

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with "CANCEL". You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Thanks,
Transform Credit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : *****************50606] Hi Monica, 

Thanks for reaching out.

I'd like to reassure you that we are a legitimate lender, incorporated in Delaware and based primarily out of Chicago and have been lending for over 5 years, across 16 different states which all require licensing. You can find this information on our website: https://transformcredit.com/Licenses. We are also registered with Nationwide Multistate Licensing System (NMLS), with license number 1905055.

We apologize if you’ve been misinformed about our loans. It’s made clear on our website that we always require a cosigner, which was displayed before you applied & entered any details. The broker would’ve passed you to us if they thought we were the best fit for you, based on your circumstances. Although we can’t directly control the broker’s messaging, we're sure they would’ve had your best interests in mind. 

I can confirm your loan application has been cancelled. I'm sorry we were unable to assist you on this occasion.

Have a nice day,
Eden@TransformCredit

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

Information provided to Together Loans by you:

Borrower: Tanya ******
IP: 169.254.130.1
DateTime (UTC): 02-21-2026
Stated Monthly Income: Self Employed $500,Benefits $900
Rent: $200.00
Bills: $150.00
Other Expenses: $80.00
Your Credit Commitments: $0.00
Together Loans Repayment: $137.39
To be safe: $50.00
Total available after expenditure and this loan repayment: $782.61


Yours sincerely
Together Loans Applications Dept

interactions.body: (2FA code issues BRW SMS) Hi Destiny. As a Together Loans customer, we send you lots of important information via SMS. It's really important that we have a cell number for you, and your cosigner separately, so we can keep you both up to date with crucial information! If the code is incorrect, request a new code and enter as soon as you receive it (the code expires after a short time). If delivery of your code to your cell is taking a long time, or you're not receiving it at all, try restarting your phone to refresh network connectivity and make sure you're in an area with strong coverage. Jade, Together Loans

interactions.body:
(Borrower GT Rule 1 Email) [Notification from Transform Credit] Hi John, 

Thanks for reaching out, we understand that it can seem difficult to find a cosigner at first. Don't worry though, we help people like you every day!

We're a cosigner lender, all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. A cosigner is a great sign that someone trusts you, and that we should too regardless of your credit history! If your cosigner trusts you, then we will.

A cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor or even a colleague) who is a US resident, has a 700+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time - someone who owns their home or pays a mortgage is much more likely to be accepted. Remember, as long as you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to make a payment! You might find some useful tips on how to approach finding or speaking to a potential cosigner here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans

When you have found someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/GGOQTPO3 - we'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. If you still find that you're not able to find a cosigner, please respond to this email with CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else.

Thanks, 
Jade@Transform Credit

interactions.body:
Hi John,
Thanks for reaching out. Transform Credit is a cosigner-based lender, which means all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. If you cannot find a cosigner, we can cancel your application if you prefer. You can apply again anytime! We can help you as soon as you find a cosigner. You can reply CANCEL, or use this link to continue when you are ready: https://tcrdt.com/s/ZF37A7FC

If you have any other questions, just let us know.

Thanks, Transform Credit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : *****************52403] Hi John,
 
Transform Credit always requires a cosigner, regardless of the loan that you're applying for and your credit score. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you to repay your loan and that we should too! This trust allows us to lend to you even if you've been turned down elsewhere. 

A suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 750 and a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time. Most of our borrowers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner. 

Send this link to your cosigner and they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/E7EPTORZ and if you have any more questions, please let me know.
 
I hope this helps.
 
Thanks, 
Transform Credit

interactions.body: [Raquel has taken the first step in helping you!] Sorry she doesn’t want to provide her bank information

(AI BW Bank - Why we need GT's bank Email) [RE: Raquel has taken the first step in helping you!] Hi Paola,

We need your cosigner's bank details for multiple reasons:

- To verify their identity
- To protect us and them against fraud - we pay the loan into this account to be sure the cosigner is aware of the loan
- As a secondary payment method, ONLY if you ever fail to pay

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi William, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/G9IPD43J - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Transform Credit

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is funded. [Affordability Confirmation] To John ******,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Gia, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/1PQJCT61 - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Ashleigh, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/ERFOF8UV - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

interactions.body:
Hi Robert,
 
Transform Credit requires a cosigner, regardless of the loan that you're applying for and your credit score. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you to repay your loan and that we should too! This trust allows us to lend to you even if you've been turned down elsewhere. 

A suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 750 and a good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners are more likely to be accepted). Most of our borrowers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner. 

Just send this link to your cosigner and they can register online in 2 minutes: https://rgct.io/s/113CXJTS and if you have any more questions, please let us know.
 
I hope this helps.
 
Nicole@TransformCredit

(Borrower GT Default Email) [RE:Ramessa needs to finalize their agreement here: https://tcrdt.com/s/HQVN48FI] Hi Robert, 

Here at Transform Credit, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 750
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://transformcredit.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/8RKCSR7B

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with "CANCEL". You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Thanks,
Transform Credit

[Affordability Confirmation] To Robert ******,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

interactions.body:
Hi Elyssa,
I am sorry but at this point Nicholas does not qualify as a co-signer for you. 

A suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 750 and a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time. Most of our borrowers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner.

Just send this link to your cosigner and they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/6HBY8A23. If you have any more questions, please let us know.

Thanks, 
Transform Credit

interactions.body: Hi Elyssa. While checking, I could confirm that, unfortunately, your previous cosigner has failed our final checks and therefore is not suitable for your loan. Due to data protection, I can't share any specific reason, we have sent them an email explaining the reason behind this decision. We can still pay out your $10000 once you find a suitable cosigner. Remember, a suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 700 and a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time. Most of our borrowers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner. Just use give your new cosigner this link to apply online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/6HBY8A23 or let us know if you need more help. Thanks, Transform Credit

[Affordability Confirmation] To Kimberly ******,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to charges of criminal fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

interactions.body:
[Loan Application] Hello there-
I am getting ready to send my cosigner the link to cosign for me. But
before his credit is ran we would like to know how the process works?

My cosigner has a Credit Score of 777. He will easily be approved to be my
cosigner, but what happens after he fills out the application? Do I then
have to be approved too?

Just want to know the process before he goes ahead and applies to be my
cosigner.

Thank you,
Kim ******

Also, if I am applying for a $4,000 loan, how much is deposited into my
cosigner's account?

Thank you!

[A message from Transform Credit] Hello-
I just have a few questions regarding this process. My cosigner is going to
apply now, which he will have no problem getting approved. once he is
approved, what is the next step? I called in and left a message, but have
not heard back yet.

I just want to know how it works before he applies.

Thank you

[A message from Transform Credit] I understand that I have to have a co-signer, and I DO HAVE ONE that is
more than qualified. That is not my question, and this is why I called and
left a message.
What happens after he applies and gets approved? Do I still have to get
approved, or am I approved according to the email that I received already?

Thank you!

[A message from Transform Credit] Thank you for your email. Once he is approved how long does it take to pay
out the loan?

Thank you! :)

interactions.body: [Help With Your Account] I don’t want an account with you.  Please make sure that you’re not charging me for anything.

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : *****************22116] Dear Tabitha,
 
Thank you for your contact.

We confirm that your CreditBuilder Account is already cancelled, and will be reported and updated as a closed account on your Credit File. 

The CreditBuilder is offered to everyone that applies for a loan with us as a Borrower, it is completely optional, giving you the opportunity to opt-in, or continue the loan application without setting up a CreditBuilder Account.

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan which is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments undoing the positive impact that your on time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, TransformCredit can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.
 
How are these payments reported to the Credit Reporting Agencies? In order for us to be able to report the successful payments to the credit bureaus we reflect a loan in the customer’s credit file, which is held in escrow by a corporate guarantor, hence a CreditBuilder customer never receives the loan. When the CreditBuilder Account is cancelled, this loan is then reported as successfully paid off and no money is owed to us from the customer.
  
For any other information, or further support we will be happy to help you.
 
Kind regards,
Credit Builder Team

interactions.body:
[Your Final TransformCredit Loan Payment] I have never been happier. My experience with Transform Credit has been the
worst.

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 090985GRID] Hi Tabitha, 

We are very sorry to hear this. We never want you to have a terrible experience and we aim to always provide excellent service. Please let us know if we can assist you with anything. We are here to help. 

Thank you, 
Kenishia@TransformCredit

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 090985GRID] Now that our business relationship is behind us, I wanted to take a moment
to share some honest feedback—something your company seemed to avoid
offering unless it came in the form of a threat.

Throughout the time I was your customer, I noticed a consistent tone of
hostility in your communications. Even when my account was current and in
good standing, I received aggressive, fear-inducing messages warning me of
“legal consequences” *before* a payment was even due. Not only was this
unnecessary—it was unprofessional and downright absurd.

It’s one thing to remind a customer of a due date. It’s another to treat
them like a criminal in advance. I can only assume this fear-based approach
is standard operating procedure at your company. If that’s the case, I
sincerely hope your leadership team eventually realizes that intimidation
is not a substitute for service.

Thankfully, I no longer have to deal with your team’s misplaced aggression
or tone-deaf messaging.But I thought it worth noting that your tactics
didn’t go unnoticed—and they didn’t work.

Warmest regards (with no legal threats attached),
Tabitha

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 090985GRID] Hi Tabita,

Thank you for reaching out.  We truly appreciate your feedback and understand your concerns. I’d like to take a moment to clarify our intentions with these communications.

Our aim is not to threaten but to ensure that you and the co-signer are well-informed about the loan status, including any missed payments and their potential implications. As a licensed lender, it’s our responsibility to keep both parties updated so that you can make timely and informed financial decisions. These communications are in line with state and federal regulations, which require us to keep you informed about the loan's status and its potential impact.

Each month, we send out payment reminders to help keep everyone informed. While these reminders focus on the due date, they don’t detail the consequences of non-payment beyond the collection from the co-signer, as we want to be transparent in accordance with their legal agreement.

Please rest assured that our tone is meant to be supportive, not aggressive or hostile. We genuinely want to help prevent any escalation of the account, as we understand the importance of avoiding larger liabilities for both parties.

I hope this helps clarify your concerns. Please feel free to reach out if you have any further questions or concerns.

Thank you, 

Kenishia@Transformcredit

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 090985GRID] Doesn't help at all. I have had several loans. Never been treated as poorly
as I was by your company.

My mom and I are not idiots.

[Your Transform Credit Loan Application Ref: *****************00308] Hi Melissa,

Thanks for choosing Transform Credit, we have attached your Proposed Credit Contract with this email for you to keep. This does not mean that your application has been approved, but it gives you a chance to read and understand the terms of the agreement. 

To finalize your application please complete all of the required steps online - https://transformcredit.com/hub?aref=*****************00308 

If you have any questions about our application process please visit our website https://transformcredit.com/faq

Amber @ Transform Credit

[Affordability Confirmation] To Melissa ******,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to charges of criminal fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

interactions.body: (AI GT Holding Response - UTD GT primary payer SMS) Hi Edward, you are the primary payer, meaning you've taken responsibility for this loan. Your next payment of $137.39 is due on 10/29/2025. If this date doesn't suit you, you can change it in our app here https://tcrdt.com/s/N8MP0EPX, where you can also make a payment, see your statement, change your payment date or update your payment details. You can also pay off your loan in full via the app or online here: https://tcrdt.com/s/BPW2ESLK If you still need us, reply HELP and your account manager will reply back soon. Thanks, Transform Credit

Hi Edward, we had set you as the primary payer because, for the past 3 months, you've made the payments, while attempts on Nicholas's payment methods failed. This meant that we would've debited the monthly payment from your account to prevent the loan from falling into arrears and accumulating extra interest, which is making the loan more expensive over time. As requested, you aren't the primary payer, we'll keep attempting Nicholas first, but if we can't collect, under your agreement, we'll collect from you to stop default and the full balance becoming due, which could affect your credit score. Berginie, Head of Collections, Transform Credit

interactions.body: [CreditBuilder Payment Reminder] Why taking $5 I don’t have transactions related on your company

(AI CB ORANGE - Multiple payments Email) [RE: CreditBuilder Payment Reminder] Hi Marlyn,

Thanks for getting in touch.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $10,000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score.

I can see that you signed up for a Transform CreditBuilder account when applying for a loan of $3000, on 07/28/2024. We sent you a ‘Welcome to Credit Builder’ email on sign-up, confirming your account, how it works and how you can manage it.

Your CreditBuilder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

I can see that you’ve been benefiting from your CreditBuilder account for the past 4 months already! During this time, we’ve also been reporting the CreditBuilder loan and the successful payments you’ve made back to Experian - one of the leading credit bureaus in the US. These on time, successful payments contribute positively towards your overall credit score, paving the way for better credit opportunities in the future.

You can find out more about CreditBuilder here: transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ or manage your account yourself here, by logging in with your SSN and email: www.transformcredit.com/CBServicing/login. If you wish to cancel at any point, just go to settings to do so and we'll mark the loan as settled on your behalf.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Transform Credit can still help you access funds of up to $10,000 too! At which point, the CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

If you need help, please let me know.

Many thanks,
Ben, Transform Credit

Amber @ Transform Credit

[Affordability Confirmation] To David ******,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to charges of criminal fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

interactions.body:
[danielmapp] spk_0: Hello, is this David? Hi, David, this is Daniel calling from transform-credit I can see that uh you left a voicemail a little bit earlier today. Excellent. Just before we go into any details to security, can I just get you to confirm your date of birth
spk_1: Please? 19.
spk_0: Excellent, thank you very much. So uh David, I can see that. Yeah, the voicemail that you left us uh was just obviously let us know that the card that we had has been closed. Uh obviously you've had some Fraud on your account. So the account that we have on file is the one that ends in 3543, is that the account your reference to
spk_1: correct?
spk_0: Right, okay. Uh So
spk_1: let me ask
spk_0: you this are
spk_1: you able to take it directly out of my account without
spk_0: the card? Yeah, yeah, we could do that. So the reason that we ask for your card is just more of a secondary payment option. So in the event that we attempt to take it from your bank account and it failed attempted to take it from your card would be the next option. And the reason we have it is because car payments are quicker and more likely to be successful,
spk_1: that that that that gives me a lot of relief because I was thinking of my card,
spk_0: so I completely see we're coming from, well, not to worry, David, uh when you get your new card just let us know so we can obviously updated, but yeah, for the time being Payment will simply just come out to your bank account uh, and also be aware that it may not necessarily show on your bank statements is being taken tomorrow. It does generally take a few days for it to clear both on your end and on our
spk_1: appreciate you clearing that
spk_0: up from David. Anything else I can help you with
spk_1: that?
spk_0: Alright, well, thank you very much for your time. Have a nice day. Okay.

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 072080COOL] Hi Angel, 

We have been attempting to contact you regarding your account, which is currently 2 days in arrears, with an outstanding payment of $237.47. 

You must address this past-due amount as soon as possible to prevent the account from falling further past due. Failure to clear the outstanding arrears could result in the account being handed over to our litigation department for further action.  

Thank you for your attention to this matter.  

Best regards, Transform Credit

interactions.body:
Hi David, I'm sorry to hear about your recent job loss I am glad to hear you have some interviews. Today, we attempted to collect the payment as scheduled, but it seems that it may not be successful. I understand that you're considering deferring the payment, but that won't be possible in this case. As you know, this is a dual liability loan, and in situations like this, we expect Angel to step in and fulfill the payment obligation as agreed upon before the loan was disbursed. Deferring the payment would only extend the loan term, resulting in increased interest and ultimately making the loan more expensive for both of you. To keep the loan from falling into arrears and prevent the full balance from becoming due, we need to receive the monthly payment of $237.47. This is essential to avoid any legal escalation, such as litigation against both of you for the full balance owed. Best regards,
Berginie@TransformCredit

interactions.body:
[Need a 10-day Payoff Letter for HELOC Application] Hi Transform Credit,

I'm a co-signer for a loan issued to David ******. I have paid almost all
of the payments for this loan, and would like to pay it off as part of a
HELOC application. Can you please provide me with a 10-day payoff letter?

I attempted to find the loan number on your website, but only got a message
on my screen that said you were working on paying off my loan and you'd get
in touch with me.

My name is Angel David ****** and again, I co-signed a loan for David
******.

Thanks so much,

David

(AI GT Holding Response - UTD GT primary payer Email) [RE: Need a 10-day Payoff Letter for HELOC Application] Hi Angel,  You are the primary payer, meaning you've taken responsibility for this loan. Your next payment of $237.47 is due on 12/15/2024. If this date doesn't suit you, you can change it in our app here https://tcrdt.com/s/N8MP0EPX, where you can also make a payment, see your statement, change your payment date or update your payment details. You can also pay off your loan in full via the app or online here: https://tcrdt.com/s/BU8OTWWH If you still need us, reply HELP and your account manager will reply back soon.  Thanks, Transform Credit
[Need a 10-day Payoff Letter for HELOC Application] HELP

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 072080COOL] Hi Angel, it seems like you tried to get in touch with us but couldn't reach us. Please remember that you can reach us through email or text message. Let us know how we can assist you by replying to this message. Best regards, Transform Credit.

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 072080COOL] Hi Transform Credit,

I'm trying to secure a 10-day payoff letter from you. Can you please help
me with this?

Thank you,

David

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 072080COOL] Hi Angel 
We do not provide 10 day payoff letters but here is a settlement which is good for 5 days https://tcrdt.com/s/V9HV04AP
Thanks 
Darrell@TransformCredit

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 072080COOL] Thanks, Darrell, but I'm afraid this is all I see when I click that link:

[image: Screenshot 2024-11-18 at 10.35.30 PM.png]

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 072080COOL] Hi Angel,

Im resending here : https://tcrdt.com/s/11EY2VPO . Let me know if you're still experiencing issues. Victoria@TransformCredit

[Affordability Confirmation] To Jordan ******,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

interactions.body: [Welcome to Transform Credit Builder!] I do not have a co-signor so my application needs to be cancelled.

(CB CANCEL - Specific email subject lines) [RE:Welcome to Transform Credit Builder!] Hi Linda,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $5000 loan on 07/16/2024. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as payment for a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, your CreditBuilder account is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, TransformCredit can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Transform CreditBuilder here - transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ . However, I can confirm that I have canceled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. 

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 750+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. Follow this link to pick up where you left off: https://tcrdt.com/s/1V82LN7G

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
Jade @ Transform CreditBuilder

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is funded. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be canceled.
